title: Solve similar right triangles
description: Find missing side lengths in figures composed of similar right triangles.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $AD$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$AD = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into two right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{AD}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the two right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle AED$ is similar to $\triangle ABC$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle ADE \cong \angle ACB$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle A \cong \angle A$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{AD}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{AD}}{AC} &= \dfrac{DE}{CB}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{AD}}{\blueE{AD} + 2} &= \dfrac{7.2}{12}\\\\
12(\blueE{AD}) &= 7.2(\blueE{AD} + 2)\\\\
12\blueE{AD} &= 7.2\blueE{AD} + 14.4\\\\
4.8\blueE{AD} &= 14.4\\\\
\blueE{AD} &= 3
\end{align}$

### Conclusion

$AD = 3$

options.alt: Triangle A B C has a right angle at point C. Segment D E forms a right angle with side A C. Point D is on side A C and point E is on side A B. Segment B C has a length of 12 units. Segment A D has a length of question mark units. Segment D E has a length of 7.2 units. Segment C D has a length of 2 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $AB$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$AB = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into two right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{AB}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the two right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle ADE$ is similar to $\triangle ABC$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle ADE \cong \angle ABC$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle A \cong \angle A$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{AB}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{AB}}{AD} &= \dfrac{BC}{DE}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{AB}}{\blueE{AB} + 9} &= \dfrac{1}{5}\\\\
5(\blueE{AB}) &= 1(\blueE{AB} + 9)\\\\
5\blueE{AB} &= \blueE{AB} + 9\\\\
4\blueE{AB} &= 9\\\\
\blueE{AB} &= 2.25
\end{align}$

### Conclusion

$AB \approx 2.3$

options.alt: Triangle A D E has a right angle at point D. Segment D E forms a right angle with side A C. Point B is on side A D and point C is on side E A. Segment B C has a length of 1 unit.  Segment B D has a length of 9 units. Segment A B has a length of question mark units. Side D E has a length of 5 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $QT$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$QT= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into two right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{QT}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the two right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle QRS$ is similar to $\triangle QUT$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle QSR \cong \angle QTU$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle Q \cong \angle Q$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{QT}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{QT}}{QS} &= \dfrac{QU}{QR}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{QT}}{\blueE{QT} + 4.8} &= \dfrac{6.1}{6.1+9.2}\\\\
15.3(\blueE{QT}) &= 6.1(\blueE{QT} + 4.8)\\\\
15.3\blueE{QT} &= 6.1\blueE{QT} + 29.28\\\\
9.2\blueE{QT} &= 29.28\\\\
\blueE{QT} &\approx 3.2
\end{align}$

### Conclusion

$QT\approx 3.2$

options.alt: Triangle Q R S has a right angle at point S. Segment T U forms a right angle with side Q S. Point T is on side Q S and point U is on side Q R. Segment T S has a length of 4.8 units. Segment Q T has a length of question mark units. Segment Q U has a length of 6.1 units. Segment U R has a length of 9.2 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $AB$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$AB = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into two right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{AB}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the two right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle ADE$ is similar to $\triangle ABC$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle ADE \cong \angle ABC$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle A \cong \angle A$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{AB}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{AB}}{AD} &= \dfrac{BC}{DE}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{AB}}{\blueE{AB} + 8} &= \dfrac{6}{8}\\\\
8(\blueE{AB}) &= 6(\blueE{AB} + 8)\\\\
8\blueE{AB} &= 6\blueE{AB} + 48\\\\
2\blueE{AB} &= 48\\\\
\blueE{AB} &= 24
\end{align}$

### Conclusion

$AB =24$

options.alt: Triangle A D E has a right angle at point D. Segment B C forms a right angle with side A D. Point B is on side A D and point C is on side A E. Segment B D has a length of 8 units. Segment A B has a length of question mark units. Segment D E has a length of 8 units. Segment C B has a length of 6 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $JN$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$JN= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into two right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{JN}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the two right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle JKL$ is similar to $\triangle JNM$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle JLK \cong \angle JMN$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle J \cong \angle J$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{JN}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{JN}}{JK} &= \dfrac{JM}{JL}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{JN}}{\blueE{JN} + 6.9} &= \dfrac{4.4}{4.4+3.6}\\\\
8(\blueE{JN}) &= 4.4(\blueE{JN} + 6.9)\\\\
8\blueE{JN} &= 4.4\blueE{JN} + 30.36\\\\
3.6\blueE{JN} &= 30.36\\\\
\blueE{JN} &\approx 8.4
\end{align}$

### Conclusion

$JN\approx 8.4$

options.alt: Triangle J K L has a right angle at point L. Segment M N forms a right angle with side J L. Point N is on side J K and point M is on side J L. Segment N K has a length of 6.9 units. Segment J M has a length of 4.4 units. Segment M L has a length of 3.6 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $VZ$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$VZ= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into two right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{VZ}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the two right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle VWX$ is similar to $\triangle VZY$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle VXW \cong \angle VYZ$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle V \cong \angle V$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{VZ}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{VZ}}{VW} &= \dfrac{ZY}{WX}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{VZ}}{\blueE{VZ} + 6.1} &= \dfrac{7.8}{13}\\\\
13(\blueE{VZ}) &= 7.8(\blueE{VZ} + 6.1)\\\\
13\blueE{VZ} &= 7.8\blueE{VZ} + 47.58\\\\
5.2\blueE{VZ} &= 47.58\\\\
\blueE{VZ} &\approx 9.2
\end{align}$

### Conclusion

$VZ\approx 9.2$

options.alt: Triangle V W X has a right angle at point X. Segment Y Z forms a right angle with side V X. Point Z is on side V W and point Y is on side V X. Segment Z W has a length of 6.1 units. Segment Y Z has a length of 7.8 units. Segment X W has a length of 13 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $RT$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$RT = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into two right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{RT}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the two right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle RWV$ is similar to $\triangle RTS$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle WVR \cong \angle TSR$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle R \cong \angle R$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{RT}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{RT}}{RW} &= \dfrac{TS}{WV}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{RT}}{6.8} &= \dfrac{4.4}{3.3}\\\\
\blueE{RT} &= \dfrac{4.4 \cdot 6.8}{3.3}\\\\
\blueE{RT} &\approx 9.07
\end{align}$

### Conclusion

$RT \approx 9.1$

options.alt: Triangle R T S has a right angle at point S. Segment V W forms a right angle with side R S. Point V is on side R S and point W is on side R T. Segment T S has a length of 4.4 units. Segment V W has a length of 3.3 units. Segment R W has a length of 6.8 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $MK$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$MK= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into two right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{MK}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the two right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle BMK$ is similar to $\triangle HMZ$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle BKM \cong \angle HZM$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle M \cong \angle M$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{MK}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{MK}}{MZ}&=\dfrac{BK}{HZ}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{MK}}{4.4} &= \dfrac{6.1}{2.4}\\\\
\blueE{MK} &= \dfrac{4.4 \cdot 6.1}{2.4}\\\\
\blueE{MK} &\approx 11.2
\end{align}$

### Conclusion

$MK \approx 11.2$

options.alt: Triangle B M K has a right angle at point K. Segment Z H forms a right angle with side M K. Point H is on side B M and point Z is on side M K. Segment B K has a length of 6.1 units. Segment H Z has a length of 2.4 units. Segment M Z has a length of 4.4 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $AC$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$AC= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into two right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{AC}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the two right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle CGA$ is similar to $\triangle DGK$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle GAC \cong \angle GKD$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle G \cong \angle G$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{AC}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{AC}}{KD}&=\dfrac{CG}{DG}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{AC}}{6} &= \dfrac{3.2+12.7}{12.7}\\\\
\blueE{AC} &= \dfrac{6 \cdot 15.9}{12.7}\\\\
\blueE{AC} &\approx 7.5
\end{align}$

### Conclusion

$AC \approx 7.5$

options.alt: Triangle C G A has a right angle at point A. Segment K D forms a right angle with side G A. Point K is on side G A and point D is on side C G. Segment K D has a length of 6 units. Segment D G has a length of 12.7 units. Segment C D has a length of 3.2 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $MS$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$MS= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into two right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{MS}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the two right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle JTL$ is similar to $\triangle JSM$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle JTL \cong \angle JSM$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle J \cong \angle J$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{MS}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{MS}}{LT}&=\dfrac{MJ}{LJ}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{MS}}{8.3} &= \dfrac{6.9}{6.9+12.9}\\\\
\blueE{MS} &= \dfrac{6.9 \cdot 8.3}{19.8}\\\\
\blueE{MS} &\approx 2.9
\end{align}$

### Conclusion

$MS \approx 2.9$

options.alt: Triangle J T L has a right angle at point T. Segment M S forms a right angle with side J T. Point S is on side J T and point M is on side L J. Segment T L has a length of 8.3 units. Segment L M has a length of 12.9 units. Segment M J has a length of 6.9 units.

**In the following triangle, find length $WP$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$WP= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into two right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{WP}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the two right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle BPI$ is similar to $\triangle WPN$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle PIB \cong \angle PNW$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle P \cong \angle P$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{WP}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{WP}}{BP}&=\dfrac{WN}{BI}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{WP}}{29.2} &= \dfrac{10.9}{16.7}\\\\
\blueE{WP} &= \dfrac{10.9 \cdot 29.2}{16.7}\\\\
\blueE{WP} &\approx 19.1

### Conclusion

$WP \approx 19.1$

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $FC$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$FC= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into two right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{FC}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the two right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle VFY$ is similar to $\triangle QFC$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle FYV\cong \angle FCQ$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle F \cong \angle F$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{FC}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{FC}}{FY}&=\dfrac{QC}{VY}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{FC}}{21.5} &= \dfrac{4.1}{13.8}\\\\
\blueE{FC} &= \dfrac{4.1 \cdot 21.5}{13.8}\\\\
\blueE{FC} &\approx 6.4
\end{align}$

### Conclusion

$FC\approx 6.4$

options.alt: Triangle V F Y has a right angle at point Y. Segment Q C forms a right angle with side F Y. Point C is on side F Y and point Q is on side V F. Segment Y V has a length of 13.8 units. Segment Q C has a length of 4.1 units. Segment F Y has a length of 21.5 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $PM$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$PM= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into two right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{PM}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the two right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle LMN$ is similar to $\triangle LPO$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle LNM\cong \angle LOP$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle L \cong \angle L$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{PM}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{LP+\blueE{PM}}{LP} &= \dfrac{NM}{OP}\\\\
\dfrac{13.8+\blueE{PM}}{13.8} &= \dfrac{17}{11.9}\\\\
11.9(13.8+\blueE{PM}) &= 234.6\\\\
164.22+11.9\blueE{PM} &= 234.6\\\\
11.9\blueE{PM} &= 70.38\\\\
\blueE{PM} &\approx 5.9
\end{align}$

### Conclusion

$PM\approx 5.9$

options.alt: Triangle L M N has a right angle at point N. Segment O P forms a right angle with side L N. Point O is on side L N and point P is on side L M. Segment M N has a length of 17 units. Segment P O has a length of 11.9 units. Segment L P has a length of 13.8 units. Segment P M has a length of question mark units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $EH$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$EH= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into two right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{EH}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the two right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle DEF$ is similar to $\triangle DHG$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle DFE\cong \angle DGH$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle D \cong \angle D$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{EH}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{DH+\blueE{EH}}{DH} &= \dfrac{DG+GF}{DG}\\\\
\dfrac{8.7+\blueE{EH}}{8.7} &= \dfrac{4.8+7.2}{4.8}\\\\
4.8(8.7+\blueE{EH}) &= 104.4\\\\
41.76+4.8\blueE{EH} &= 104.4\\\\
4.8\blueE{EH} &= 62.64\\\\
\blueE{EH} &\approx 13.1
\end{align}$

### Conclusion

$EH\approx 13.1$

options.alt: Triangle D E F has a right angle at point F. Segment G H forms a right angle with side D F. Point G is on side D F and point H is on side D E. Segment D G has a length of 4.8 units. Segment G F has a length of 7.2 units. Segment D H has a length of 8.7 units. Segment E H has a length of question mark units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $CD$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$CD = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into three right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{CD}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the three right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle DBA$ is similar to $\triangle ABC$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle ADB \cong \angle CAB$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle B \cong \angle B$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

$\triangle DAC$ is similar to $\triangle ABC$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle CDA \cong \angle CAB$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle C \cong \angle C$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

Both smaller triangles—$\triangle DBA$ and $\triangle DAC$—are similar to the larger triangle $\triangle ABC$. Therefore $\triangle DBA$ and $\triangle DAC$ are also similar to each other.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{CD}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{CD}}{CA} &= \dfrac{AC}{BC} \\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{CD}}{8} &= \dfrac{8}{\blueE{CD}+12.2} \\\\
\blueE{CD}(\blueE{CD} + 12.2) &= 64\\\\
(\blueE{CD})^2 + 12.2(\blueE{CD})  &= 64\\\\
(\blueE{CD})^2 + 12.2(\blueE{CD}) + (6.1)^2 &= 64 +(6.1)^2& \text{Complete the square.}\\\\
(\blueE{CD} + 6.1)^2 &= 101.21\\\\
\blueE{CD} + 6.1 &\approx \pm 10.06\\\\
\blueE{CD} &\approx \pm 10.06 - 6.1
\end{align}$

Only the positive side length makes sense, so $\blueE{CD}$ is about $3.96$ units long.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

### Conclusion

$CD \approx 4$

options.alt: Triangle C B A has a right angle at point A. Segment A D forms a right angle with the hypotenuse. Segment C A has a length of 8 units. Segment C D has a length of question mark units. Segment B D has a length of 12.2 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $KL$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$KL= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into three right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{KL}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the three right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle KRP$ is similar to $\triangle LRK$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle PKR\cong \angle KLR$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle R \cong \angle R$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

$\triangle KRP$ is similar to $\triangle LKP$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle PKR\cong \angle PLK$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle P \cong \angle P$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

Both smaller triangles—$\triangle LRK$ and $\triangle LKP$—are similar to the larger triangle $\triangle KRP$. Therefore $\triangle LRK$ and $\triangle LKP$ are also similar to each other.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{KL}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{KL}}{PL}&=\dfrac{LR}{LK} \\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{KL}}{7.5} &= \dfrac{11.7}{\blueE{KL}} \\\\
(\blueE{KL})^2 &= 87.75\\\\
\sqrt{(\blueE{KL})^2} &= \sqrt{87.75}& \text{Take the square root.}\\\\
\blueE{KL} &\approx \pm 9.37
\end{align}$

Only the positive side length makes sense, so $\blueE{KL}$ is about $9.37$ units long.

### Conclusion

$KL \approx 9.4$

options.alt: Triangle K P R has a right angle at point K. Segment K L forms a right angle with the hypotenuse P R. Segment P L has a length of 7.5 units. Segment K L has a length of question mark units. Segment L R has a length of 11.7 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $AE$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$AE= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into three right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{AE}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the three right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle IEU$ is similar to $\triangle AEI$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle UIE\cong \angle IAE$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle E \cong \angle E$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

$\triangle IEU$ is similar to $\triangle AIU$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle UIE\cong \angle UAI$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle U \cong \angle U$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

Both smaller triangles—$\triangle AEI$ and $\triangle AIU$—are similar to the larger triangle $\triangle IEU$. Therefore $\triangle AEI$ and $\triangle AIU$ are also similar to each other.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{AE}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{AE}}{IE}&=\dfrac{EI}{EU} \\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{AE}}{24} &= \dfrac{24}{\blueE{AE}+8.4} \\\\
\blueE{AE}(\blueE{AE} + 8.4) &= 576\\\\
(\blueE{AE})^2 + 8.4(\blueE{AE})  &= 576\\\\
(\blueE{AE})^2 + 8.4(\blueE{AE}) + (4.2)^2 &= 576 +(4.2)^2& \text{Complete the square.}\\\\
(\blueE{AE} + 4.2)^2 &= 593.64\\\\
\blueE{AE} + 4.2&\approx \pm 24.36\\\\
\blueE{AE} &\approx \pm 24.36- 4.2
\end{align}$

Only the positive side length makes sense, so $\blueE{AE}$ is about $20.16$ units long.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

### Conclusion

$AE\approx 20.2$

options.alt: Triangle I E U has a right angle at point I. Segment A I forms a right angle with the hypotenuse E U. Segment I E has a length of 24 units. Segment A E has a length of question mark units. Segment U A has a length of 8.4 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $YV$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$YV= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into three right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{YV}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the three right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle VRY$ is similar to $\triangle LRV$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle YVR \cong \angle VLR$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle R \cong \angle R$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

$\triangle VRY$ is similar to $\triangle LVY$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle YVR \cong \angle VLY$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle Y \cong \angle Y$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

Both smaller triangles—$\triangle LRV$ and $\triangle LVY$—are similar to the larger triangle $\triangle VRY$. Therefore $\triangle LRV$ and $\triangle LVY$ are also similar to each other.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{YV}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{YV}}{YL}&=\dfrac{RY}{VY} \\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{YV}}{10.3} &= \dfrac{17.3+10.3}{\blueE{YV}} \\\\
(\blueE{YV})^2 &= 284.28\\\\
\sqrt{(\blueE{YV})^2} &= \sqrt{284.28}& \text{Take the square root.}\\\\
\blueE{YV} &\approx\pm 16.86
\end{align}$

Only the positive side length makes sense, so $\blueE{YV}$ is about $16.86$ units long.

### Conclusion

$YV \approx 16.9$

options.alt: Triangle V R Y has a right angle at point V. Segment V L forms a right angle with the hypotenuse. Segment R L has a length of 17.3 units. Segment Y V has a length of question mark units. Segment L Y has a length of 10.3 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $HD$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$HD= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into three right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{HD}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the three right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle HDG$ is similar to $\triangle BDH$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle GHD\cong \angle HBD$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle D \cong \angle D$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

$\triangle HDG$ is similar to $\triangle BHG$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle GHD\cong \angle GBH$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle G \cong \angle G$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

Both smaller triangles—$\triangle BDH$ and $\triangle BHG$—are similar to the larger triangle $\triangle HDG$. Therefore $\triangle BDH$ and $\triangle BHG$ are also similar to each other.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{HD}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{HD}}{DG}&=\dfrac{BD}{DH} \\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{HD}}{14+18} &= \dfrac{18}{\blueE{HD}} \\\\
(\blueE{HD})^2 &= 576\\\\
\sqrt{(\blueE{HD})^2} &= \sqrt{576}& \text{Take the square root.}\\\\
\blueE{HD} &=\pm 24
\end{align}$

Only the positive side length makes sense, so $\blueE{HD}$ is about $24$ units long.

### Conclusion

$HD = 24$

options.alt: Triangle H D G has a right angle at point H. Segment B H forms a right angle with the hypotenuse D G. Segment G B has a length of 14 units. Segment H D has a length of question mark units. Segment B D has a length of 18 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $BW$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$BW= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into three right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{BW}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the three right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle NSB$ is similar to $\triangle WSN$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle BNS \cong \angle NWS$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle S \cong \angle S$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

$\triangle NSB$ is similar to $\triangle WNB$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle BNS \cong \angle BWN$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle B \cong \angle B$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

Both smaller triangles—$\triangle WSN$ and $\triangle WNB$—are similar to the larger triangle $\triangle NSB$. Therefore $\triangle WSN$ and $\triangle WNB$ are also similar to each other.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{BW}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{BW}}{BN}&=\dfrac{NB}{SB} \\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{BW}}{12} &= \dfrac{12}{\blueE{BW}+16.1} \\\\
\blueE{BW}(\blueE{BW} + 16.1) &= 144\\\\
(\blueE{BW})^2 + 16.1(\blueE{BW})  &= 144\\\\
(\blueE{BW})^2 + 16.1(\blueE{BW}) + (8.05)^2 &= 144 +(8.05)^2& \text{Complete the square.}\\\\
(\blueE{BW} + 8.05)^2 &= 208.8025\\\\
\blueE{BW} + 8.05&= \pm 14.45\\\\
\blueE{BW} &= \pm 14.45 - 8.05
\end{align}$

Only the positive side length makes sense, so $\blueE{BW}$ is about $6.4$ units long.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

### Conclusion

$BW \approx 6.4$

options.alt: Triangle N S B has a right angle at point N. Segment N W forms a right angle with the hypotenuse. Segment N B has a length of 12 units. Segment B W has a length of question mark units. Segment W S has a length of 16.1 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $WY$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$WY = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into three right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{WY}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the three right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle WYZ$ is similar to $\triangle WXY$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle YZW \cong \angle XYW$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle W \cong \angle W$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

$\triangle YXZ$ is similar to $\triangle WXY$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle XZY \cong \angle XYW$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle X \cong \angle X$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

Both smaller triangles—$\triangle WYZ$ and $\triangle YXZ$—are similar to the larger triangle $\triangle WXY$. Therefore $\triangle WYZ$ and $\triangle YXZ$ are also similar to each other.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{WY}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{WY}}{YZ} &= \dfrac{YX}{ZX}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{WY}}{5.7} &=\dfrac{7}{4}\\\\
\blueE{WY} &= \dfrac{7 \cdot 5.7}{4}\\\\
\blueE{WY} &= 9.975
\end{align}$

### Conclusion

$WY \approx 10.0$

options.alt: Triangle W X Y has a right angle at point Y. Segment Y Z forms a right angle with the hypotenuse. Segment W Y has a length of question mark units. Segment X Y has a length of 7 units. Segment X Z has a length of 4 units. Segment Y Z has a length of 5.7 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $EL$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$EL= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into three right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{EL}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the three right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle EHL$ is similar to $\triangle PHE$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle LEH\cong \angle EPH$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle H \cong \angle H$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

$\triangle EHL$ is similar to $\triangle PEL$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle LEH\cong \angle LPE$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle L \cong \angle L$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

Both smaller triangles—$\triangle PHE$ and $\triangle PEL$—are similar to the larger triangle $\triangle EHL$. Therefore $\triangle PHE$ and $\triangle PEL$ are also similar to each other.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{EP}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{EL}}{PE} &= \dfrac{HE}{HP}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{EL}}{9.7} &=\dfrac{21}{18.6}\\\\
\blueE{EL} &= \dfrac{21 \cdot 9.7}{18.6}\\\\
\blueE{EL} &\approx 10.95
\end{align}$

### Conclusion

$EL\approx 11.0$

options.alt: Triangle E H L has a right angle at point E. Segment E P forms a right angle with the hypotenuse and has a length of 9.7 units. Segment P H has a length of 18.6 units. Segment E H has a length of 21 units. Segment L E has a length of question mark units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $MV$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$MV= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into three right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{MV}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the three right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle VDM$ is similar to $\triangle QDV$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle MVD \cong \angle VQD$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle D \cong \angle D$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

$\triangle VDM$ is similar to $\triangle QVM$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle MVD \cong \angle MQV$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle M \cong \angle M$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

Both smaller triangles—$\triangle QDV$ and $\triangle QVM$—are similar to the larger triangle $\triangle VDM$. Therefore $\triangle QDV$ and $\triangle QVM$ are also similar to each other.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{MV}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{MV}}{VQ}&=\dfrac{VD}{QD}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{MV}}{13.8} &=\dfrac{23}{18.4}\\\\
\blueE{MV} &= \dfrac{23 \cdot 13.8}{18.4}\\\\
\blueE{MV} &= 17.25
\end{align}$

### Conclusion

$MV\approx 17.3$

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $BD$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$BD= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into three right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{BD}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the three right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle CAB$ is similar to $\triangle DCB$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle BCA \cong \angle BDC$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle B \cong \angle B$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

$\triangle CAB$ is similar to $\triangle DAC$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle BCA \cong \angle CDA$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle A \cong \angle A$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

Both smaller triangles—$\triangle DCB$ and $\triangle DAC$—are similar to the larger triangle $\triangle CAB$. Therefore $\triangle DCB$ and $\triangle DAC$ are also similar to each other.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{BD}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{\blueE{BD}+DA}{AC}&=\dfrac{CA}{DA}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{BD}+7.4}{9.3} &=\dfrac{9.3}{7.4}\\\\
7.4(\blueE{BD}+7.4) &= (9.3)^2\\\\
7.4\blueE{BD}+54.76 &= 86.49\\\\
7.4\blueE{BD} &= 31.73\\\\
\blueE{BD} &\approx 4.29
\end{align}$

### Conclusion

$BD \approx 4.3$

options.alt: Triangle C A B has a right angle at point C. Segment C D forms a right angle with the hypotenuse. Segment B D has a length of question mark units. Segment D A has a length of 7.4 units. Segment C A has a length of 9.3 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $TR$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$TR= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into three right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{TR}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the three right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle JRG$ is similar to $\triangle TJG$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle GJR \cong \angle GTJ$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle G \cong \angle G$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

$\triangle JRG$ is similar to $\triangle TRJ$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle GJR \cong \angle JTR$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle R \cong \angle R$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

Both smaller triangles—$\triangle TJG$ and $\triangle TRJ$—are similar to the larger triangle $\triangle JRG$. Therefore $\triangle TJG$ and $\triangle TRJ$ are also similar to each other.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{TR}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{GT+\blueE{TR}}{JG}&=\dfrac{GJ}{GT}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{TR}+2.3}{5.5} &=\dfrac{5.5}{2.3}\\\\
2.3(\blueE{TR}+2.3) &= (5.5)^2\\\\
2.3\blueE{TR}+5.29&= 30.25\\\\
2.3\blueE{TR} &= 24.96\\\\
\blueE{TR} &\approx 10.85
\end{align}$

### Conclusion

$TR \approx 10.9$

options.alt: Triangle J R G has a right angle at point J. Segment J T forms a right angle with the hypotenuse. Segment T R has a length of question mark units. Segment G J has a length of 5.5 units. Segment G T has a length of 2.3 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the following triangle, find length $YZ$.**  
*Round to the nearest tenth.*

[[☃ image 1]]

$YZ= $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### Strategy

We can decompose the figure into three right triangles. Then we can find $\blueE{YZ}$ using a proportion from similar triangles.

###Identifying similar triangles

Let's visualize the three right triangles from the figure separately.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle KWY$ is similar to $\triangle ZWK$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle YKW\cong \angle KZW$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle W \cong \angle W$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

$\triangle KWY$ is similar to $\triangle ZKY$ by angle-angle similarity:

* $\angle YKW\cong \angle YZK$ because all right angles are congruent.
* $\angle Y \cong \angle Y$ because any angle is congruent to itself.

Both smaller triangles—$\triangle ZWK$ and $\triangle ZKY$—are similar to the larger triangle $\triangle KWY$. Therefore $\triangle ZWK$ and $\triangle ZKY$ are also similar to each other.

###Solving the proportion for $\blueE{YZ}$

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{WZ+\blueE{YZ}}{WK}&=\dfrac{KW}{ZW}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{YZ}+15.9}{21} &=\dfrac{21}{15.9}\\\\
15.9(\blueE{YZ}+15.9) &= (21)^2\\\\
15.9\blueE{YZ}+252.81&= 441\\\\
15.9\blueE{YZ} &= 188.19\\\\
\blueE{YZ} &\approx 11.84
\end{align}$

### Conclusion

$YZ \approx 11.8$

options.alt: Triangle K W Y has a right angle at point K. Segment K Z forms a right angle with the hypotenuse. Segment Y Z has a length of question mark units. Segment Z W has a length of 15.9 units. Segment K W has a length of 21 units.
